title: Pod Security, Security Contexts, Images, Admission, Secrets, and Multi-Tenancy · API and Operations Recall
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs pod security, security contexts, images, admission, secrets, and multi-tenancy from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the restricted workload and admission policy under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for restricted workload and admission policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply Privileged, Baseline, and Restricted Pod Security Standards with enforce, audit, warn, version pinning, exemptions, namespace labels, and rollout evidence.
Set non-root identity, groups, filesystem ownership, read-only root, capabilities, privilege escalation, seccomp, host namespaces, devices, and writable mounts from threat evidence.
Require immutable images, provenance, signatures, vulnerability policy, registry allowlists, architecture, pull credentials, and admission decisions with exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: K8s Pod Security Standards Admission · changed case · Review K8s Pod Security Standards Admission
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Pod runs as root, is privileged, adds capabilities, mounts hostPath, permits privilege escalation, and bypasses a namespace policy exception. Enforce Pod Security, securityContext, seccomp, admission, exception ownership, and residual shared-kernel risk.

Which action demonstrates K8s Pod Security Standards Admission without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: K8s Pod Security Standards Admission · changed case · Review K8s Pod Security Standards Admission.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply privileged, baseline, and restricted pod security standards with enforce, audit, warn, version pinning, exemptions, namespace labels, and rollout evidence. For K8s Pod Security Standards Admission · changed case · Review K8s Pod Security Standards Admission, keep K8s Pod Security Standards Admission connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.
